items.body: Swamy of the same name, and starring Kalyan Kumar, Jayasimha, Leelavathi. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘I felt Holden was talking to me alone’: The Catcher in the Rye at 75
items.body:
JD Salinger’s wry, subversive classic inspired novelist Joseph O’Connor to be a writer. He reflects on why this story of a disaffected teenager remains as fresh and transgressive as ever

items.hook: Both artists sought to depict similar episodes from very early Christian history, portraying families helping an injured individual.
items.body: A Converted British Family Sheltering a Christian Missionary from the Persecution of the Druids is a painting by the English artist William Holman Hunt that was exhibited at the Royal Academy in 1850 and is now in the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford. The world is s

items.body: The jubilee edition will unspool under the motto “Show up or it doesn't count!” and feature 105 films across eight competition sections

items.body:
Looking for a new reading recommendation? Here are some fantastic new paperbacks, from the gripping story of an international murder case to a state-of-the-nation yarn

items.hook: Catherine Called Birdy is a 2022 medieval comedy film written for the screen, co-produced, and directed by Lena Dunham.
items.body: It is based on the 1994 novel of the same name by Karen Cushman. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Interview: Jessica Woodworth • Director - “Mongolians, in general, are deeply connected to nature, to their vast and powerful landscapes, and to their omnipresent nomadic culture” - /Europe/Mongolia
items.body: The Belgian-American director discusses the acting workshop she led as part of the European Film Festival organised by the European Union Delegation to Mongolia

items.hook: Extra Credit is a 2009 children's novel written by Andrew Clements.
items.body: The work was first published on June 23, 2009 through Simon & Schuster and follows a young schoolgirl who is given the option of receiving extra credit by writing to an overseas pen pal in a small Afghanistan village. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Suzanne Karpelès was a French Indologist, who was a multilingual specialist in the languages and cultures of colonized French Indochina.
items.body: She was the first curator of the Royal Library of Phnom Penh and suggested the founding of the Buddhist Institute of Cambodia where she served as the first secretary-general. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Aleek Manush is a Bengali novel by Syed Mustafa Siraj, first published in 1988.
items.body: Set in rural Murshidabad, it follows a Farazi preacher and his son, who rejects religious orthodoxy. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Rebecca Perry wins Waterstones debut fiction prize for ‘delicious and dream-like’ novel
items.body:
May We Feed the King, about a museum curator and a medieval monarch, was praised by judges for its ‘crisp, cool prose’ and ‘poet’s eye for detail’

Author and poet Rebecca Perry has won the 2026 Waterstones debut fiction prize for M
